title: Lamb Loin Chops (Cast Iron)
subtitle: Hot cast-iron sear with optional herb butter: deep crust, warm pink center around 130–135°F.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: Render trimmed fat to season the pan before the chops hit the metal.
items: Lamb loin chops (as many as fit in the pan without crowding)
Lamb fat (trimmed from the chops, for rendering in the pan)
ingredients.callout: Done when: deep brown crust, warm pink center, internal temp ~130–135°F.

body.html: A straight cast-iron sear: dry surface + hot fat builds a real crust. Optional butter and herbs at the flip add richness without stealing the lamb flavor.

items: Season and rest so the surface isn't wet
Heat the pan on trimmed lamb fat until it's seriously hot

body.html: Don't crowd. Moisture steams instead of sears—cook in batches if needed.

slides.title: Season
slides.checkboxLabel: Pat dry, salt & pepper, rest 10–15 min

items: Season with kosher salt + black pepper on both sides.

body.html: Why rest here: salt draws surface moisture; a short sit helps you sear instead of steam.

slides.title: Heat the Pan
slides.checkboxLabel: Render lamb fat until pan is hot, lightly smoking

items: Add the trimmed lamb fat and let it render until the pan is hot and lightly smoking.

body.html: No usable trim? Use a thin film of neutral oil with high smoke point, then proceed the same way.

slides.title: Sear (Side 1)
slides.checkboxLabel: Add chops; cook 3 min without moving

items: Lay chops in the pan in a single layer.

body.html: Color = flavor. Moving too early tears the crust.

slides.title: Flip + Flavor
slides.checkboxLabel: Flip; add butter & herbs; baste 30–60 sec

items: Baste the meat with the foaming butter 30–60 seconds.
Continue cooking 2–3 minutes on the second side until crust and doneness look right.

body.html: Check temp: pull around 130–135°F for warm pink center (carryover happens).

items: Rest 5 minutes before slicing or serving.

body.html: You're aiming for: deep brown crust outside, warm pink inside, ~130–135°F.
